prerequisites: A running Grafana instance reachable over HTTPS (Grafana OSS, Enterprise, or Grafana Cloud).
The base URL of your Grafana instance for the required `url` config field, for example https://your-grafana.grafana.net.
An account with permission to create service accounts: Admin rights, or the `fixed:roles:reader` and `fixed:serviceaccounts:creator` roles.
A Grafana API key or service account token for the required `api_key` config field.

authMethods.name: API key / service account token
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single `api_key` secret field. Provide a Grafana API key or a service account token. Grafana accepts it on the HTTP API as a Bearer credential in the Authorization header. On Grafana Cloud, the HTTP API is authenticated with a service account token.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Grafana instance URL
setupSteps.body: Note the base URL of the Grafana instance you want to sync, for example https://your-grafana.grafana.net. Ensure it is reachable over HTTPS. You will enter this in the required `url` config field.

setupSteps.title: Create a service account in Grafana
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Grafana and go to Administration > Users and access > Service accounts > Add service account. Enter a display name, assign an organization role (Viewer is sufficient for read-only sync; Editor or Admin for broader access), and create it. You need Admin rights, or the `fixed:roles:reader` and `fixed:serviceaccounts:creator` roles, to do this.

setupSteps.title: Generate a service account token
setupSteps.body: Open the service account, click Add service account token, enter a token name, optionally set an expiration date, then click Generate token. Copy the generated token. On Grafana Cloud the HTTP API requires a service account token.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the Grafana instance base URL in the `url` field and paste the service account token (or API key) into the `api_key` field. If you use Airbyte Cloud and your organization restricts access to specific IPs, add the Airbyte Cloud IP addresses to your allow list so it can reach your Grafana instance.

sources.title: Authentication options for the HTTP API - Grafana documentation
Grafana update: Service account tokens are replacing API keys
